exemplar.title: To what extent have migrants altered the cultural landscape in three neighborhoods in Antwerp

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have migrants altered the cultural landscape in three neighborhoods in Antwerp

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic and geographic setting are communicated clearly and returned to throughout the report
Research question is explicitly stated, geographically grounded and narrowed by subsidiary hypotheses
A generally relevant set of primary and secondary methods is employed (transects, photographic survey, demographic and cartographic sources)
weaknesses: Occasional wordiness and tangential background blur the focus
Justification for key methodological choices (sample size, transect spacing, operational definitions) is brief or deferred
Links between sub-hypotheses and the central question are not always sustained in later analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates topic, question and methods adequately, with clear purpose and mostly focused RQ. However, it lacks the precision and fully defended methodology required for the top band, placing it solidly in the 3-4 range. A mid-band mark of 3 reflects the effective but not consistently robust focus and method.

strengths: Key geographical concepts (cultural diffusion, acculturation, hybridization) are defined and applied to Antwerp
Appropriate maps and demographic data demonstrate spatial understanding
Terminology is mostly accurate and figures are properly labelled
weaknesses: Theoretical engagement remains largely descriptive in places and some historical claims lack precise sourcing
Terminology use is occasionally inconsistent or imprecise (e.g. language identification errors)
Source integration is adequate but not critically analysed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge of subject content and use of terminology are sound but uneven. The student demonstrates clear understanding and effective use of sources overall, yet falls short of the critical depth and consistency of the top band. A mid-band mark of 3 accurately reflects this level of performance.

strengths: Primary and secondary data are integrated with clear relevance to each hypothesis
Maps and correlations illustrate links between migrant concentrations and culturalâlandscape indicators
Argument structure is logical and signposted, guiding the reader through hypotheses and conclusions
weaknesses: Statistical methods (Spearmanâs rank) are applied without full critical discussion of assumptions or limitations
Analysis occasionally relies on untriangulated observations and contains unsupported leaps
Counter-arguments and alternative explanations are mentioned only briefly and not fully explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs application of research is solid and generally relevant, analysis produces mostly supported conclusions, and the argument is coherent with only minor inconsistencies. These strengths place the work in the mid-range of the 4-6 band for critical thinking. A mark of 6 reflects the competent but not consistently rigorous evaluation.

strengths: Report structure follows IB conventions with clear sectioning, figure numbering and captions
Consistent formatting of font, spacing and margins enhances readability
Visual elements (maps, tables, photographs) are well integrated into the text
weaknesses: Occasional caption duplication and inconsistent citation formatting
Some tables interrupt narrative flow and would be better placed in appendices
Minor layout issues (pixelated images, inconsistent numeric notation) detract slightly from presentation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The organization and layout largely facilitate understanding, and most presentation conventions are met. However, recurrent minor lapses in captioning, table placement and referencing prevent a top-band score. A mark of 3 appropriately reflects the clear but imperfect presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student gathers primary data (field observations, photographic evidence, simple counts) and integrates it with secondary statistics to address each hypothesis. Links between data collected and the research question are **clear and generally relevant**, exemplified by maps that overlay survey points with immigrant concentrations and by the use of Spearmanâs rank to test relationships.  However, the explanatory power of some datasets is limited (e.g., small sample size, lack of triangulation), and certain findings are asserted rather than demonstrated.  Thus the research application is solid but not consistently sophisticated, meriting the mid-band.

The student combines primary fieldwork (stratified/systematic transects, photographic survey, Spearmanâs rank tests) with secondary demographic, cartographic and theoretical sources.  This constitutes a **generally relevant and workable set of methods**.  However, justification of choices (e.g., why 20 points, why 100-m spacing, why particular variables in the correlations) is brief, and some techniques (e.g., Spearmanâs rank with very small samples) are applied uncritically.  Limitations are acknowledged only later in the Evaluation section.  Consequently the methodology is âmostly completeâ rather than consistently appropriate and fully defended.

The essay shows **sound but uneven geographical knowledge**.  Key conceptsâcultural/relocation diffusion, acculturation strategies, cultural landscapeâare defined and applied to Antwerpâs migrant groups.  Maps (ArcGIS) and demographic data demonstrate an understanding of spatial patterns.  Yet theoretical engagement is sometimes descriptive; certain explanations (e.g., conflating linguistic landscape with assimilation) lack depth, and some historic detail is broad or unsourced.  Source use is adequate but not always critically integrated, limiting the demonstration of âexcellentâ knowledge.

The visual presentation is neat: consistent font, spacing, margins and an extensive use of maps, tables and photographs.  Captions, legends, scale bars and north arrows are usually present.  However, some images are slightly pixelated, a few captions are duplicated, and tables run across pages awkwardly.  Referencing style is applied but inconsistently (e.g., mixed punctuation, occasional missing access dates).  Overall, layout considerations are **mostly correct and enhance readability**, but lapses mean the work does not fully meet the highest standard.

Geographical terms such as âacculturation,â âhierarchical diffusion,â âethnic enclaveâ and âcontagious diffusionâ are employed appropriately through most sections, and figures are labelled with conventions (scale bars, north arrows).  Nevertheless, occasional inaccuracies occur (e.g., referring to Yiddish as âYiddish languageâ alongside Dutch on a mosque sign, or mis-labelling Yiddish as âYiddish and Dutchâ), and some terminology (e.g., âhybrid cultureâ vs. âintegrationâ) is used loosely.  Consistency and precision fall short of the top band, but the overall usage is **mostly accurate and shows reasonable command of concepts**.

The student states the topic and its geographic setting from the outset and returns to it in the introduction, analysis and conclusion. Headings, maps and figures clearly signal the three chosen neighbourhoods and the focus on cultural-landscape change.  Explanations of key ideas such as cultural diffusion, acculturation and hybridity are provided, so the reader can follow the line of inquiry without difficulty.  Minor issuesâe.g., occasional wordiness and some tangential backgroundâslightly blur the focus, preventing the outstanding precision required for the top band, but overall communication is **adequate-to-good and purposeful**.

The essay presents a logical structureâintroduction, hypotheses, analysis per hypothesis, conclusionâand makes an effort to build an argument that migrants have significantly reshaped Antwerpâs cultural landscape.  Paragraphing and signposting help the reader follow the discussion.  However, **the line of argument occasionally drifts into description**, and some claims (e.g., âintegration evident in all high-migrant neighbourhoodsâ) are not fully reconciled with the data.  Counter-arguments and limitations are mentioned in passing but not explored deeply.  These factors place the discussion in the competent but not consistently convincing band.

The analysis links linguistic landscapes, services and demographic data to degrees of assimilation, and employs Spearmanâs rank to explore correlations.  Conclusions (e.g., weaker assimilation of the Jewish diaspora) are **mostly supported** by evidence such as percentage calculations and mapped distributions.  Nonetheless, statistical treatment is basic; confidence levels are quoted but critical comment on the validity of the tests, potential biases and alternative explanations is limited.  Some analytical leaps (e.g., âlanguage barriers hinder hybridityâ) are asserted without full evidential backing.  Overall, analysis is competent yet not rigorous enough for the higher bands.

The report follows IB EE conventions: title page, abstract omitted but word count given, contents page, logical sectioning, clear figure numbering and captions.  The sequence largely aids comprehension, and figures are embedded near relevant text.  Minor issues include occasional redundancy in headings, some mis-placement of long captions and data tables relegated to the main text rather than appendices, which interrupts flow.  These issues prevent a wholly seamless structure, yet overall **the organisation is clear and facilitates understanding**.

The research questionââTo what extent have migrants altered the cultural landscape in three neighbourhoods in Antwerp?ââis explicitly stated, geographically grounded and narrow enough to be addressed within 4â000 words.  Subsidiary hypotheses (H1-H3) give additional structure.  Nonetheless, the phrasing "to what extent" across three heterogeneous districts still leaves scope broad, and the links between the sub-hypotheses and the central question are not always sustained in the later discussion.  Hence the question is **clearly stated and mostly focused, but not yet exemplary in precision or alignment with all later analysis.**

annotations.comment: Model diagrams are helpful, but a critical discussion of their limitations and assumptions in the Antwerp context would strengthen your evaluation.
Cultural diffusion is defined appropriately, but you should integrate a brief example here to show its direct relevance to Antwerpâs migrant dynamics.
Berryâs acculturation model is used correctly, but ensure terminology accuracyâverify that language examples (e.g. Yiddish vs Arabic) are correctly identified.
The survey form captures key elements, but lacks instructions for ensuring inter-observer reliability and consistency across field visits.
The choropleth map uses comma decimal separators (e.g. â28,5â); consider consistent numeric notation to enhance readability and avoid confusion.
The title of the investigation is clearly stated at the start, satisfying the IB requirement for declaring the research focus up front.
The location map of Antwerp is well labelled with north arrow and scale bar, effectively situating the study area for the reader.
The word count is provided and remains under the 4 000-word limit, meeting the formal IB formatting requirement.
The introduction states the research question and context clearly but would benefit from previewing the analytical approach or structure to strengthen the focus and guide the reader.
Caption formatting is inconsistent; include access dates and follow a uniform citation style to improve presentation and traceability of online resources.
Historical context is presented, but claims such as âglobal Diamond Capital for over 500 yearsâ require precise sourcing and deeper analysis to demonstrate excellent subject knowledge.
Survey area maps are clear, but the methodology should explicitly justify the choice of transect locations and spacing to strengthen methodological transparency.
The photograph of the Diamond District effectively illustrates cultural-pull factors, enriching the geographical context with visual evidence.
Diffusion models are introduced clearly, yet the analysis lacks direct linkage to evidence from your Brussels fieldworkâexplain how each diffusion type manifests in your case study.
Hypotheses are stated clearly, but âassimilated moreâ in H1 needs operational definitions (e.g. criteria for assimilation) to ensure measurable testing.
Site selection based on âpersonal knowledgeâ indicates limited justification; provide empirical or secondary-data criteria to support neighborhood choices.
Google Maps identification is valuable, but describe your on-site verification process to confirm that digital labels correspond to real-world features.
You note that analysis addresses H3, but operational criteria for âcultural hybridityâ should be defined in the methodology to ensure consistent measurement.
Stratified and systematic sampling is described, yet the choice of 20 data points and 100 m intervals requires explicit rationale and justification.
Spearmanâs rank is appropriate, but discuss how you handled tied ranks and whether assumptions for ordinal data are met before interpreting results.
The full correlation table is informative but interrupts narrative flow; consider moving detailed tables to an appendix and summarizing key statistics in text.
Inferring that North African migrants have assimilated more than Jewish diaspora based on a single sign is not robustâtriangulate with service-use or survey data for stronger evaluation.
Reporting of the Spearman coefficient is clear, but discuss the practical interpretation of a weak negative correlation in terms of assimilation dynamics.
Population distribution map is well designed, but explain the rationale behind threshold values for each color class in your legend.
Historical map of Jewish areas provides context, but the text should link these historic settlement patterns explicitly to modern assimilation analysis.
Figure legend text âdutchâ is not capitalized; ensure consistent capitalization and terminology across all map labels and captions.
Language identification on the mosque sign is inconsistent between text (âArabicâ) and caption (âYiddishâ); verify with native speakers for accurate analysis.
Annotated map of Jewish services is compelling, but quantify service density relative to population to strengthen the argument for separation strategy.
Confidence level table is thorough but could be condensed to show only the threshold for n=20 to maintain reader focus on relevant statistics.
Good identification of varied assimilation levels, but consider exploring alternative explanations (e.g. policy, economic integration) in your discussion.
Your review of hybridity definitions is comprehensive yet largely descriptive; include a critical assessment of each conceptâs fit for Antwerpâs multi-ethnic context.
Reflection on methodology is mostly descriptive; deepen your evaluation by linking setbacks and limitations directly to decisions made and proposing remedial actions.
The conclusion effectively restates findings, but could be more concise and integrate an overarching argument on the studyâs contribution to geographical understanding.
